AI-Powered Observability
& Automation

Your DevOps from day one.

The AIOps platform for everything in production — from microservices to GPU fleets and ML pipelines. AI triage in ~15 seconds, runbooks across pods and VM fleets, and FinOps that cuts your Kubernetes, AWS and GPU bill in half.

No credit card· Bring your own model (BYOM) for regulated environments· Live in 1 day
app.alertmend.io / incidents / log-ingester
Incident #8472 Firing

log-ingester

CrashLoopBackOff · 257 restarts
Cluster
prod-us-east-1
Namespace
observability
Repo
acme/log-ingester
Source
Datadog·Prometheus
Fired
2 min ago
On-call
@alex (auto-paged)
Recent activity
  • 2mPod evicted · ephemeral-storage
  • 9mRestart attempt #256
  • 14mDeploy v2.31.4
AI Root Cause AnalysisResolved in 14.2s
  • Pulled pod events, logs, metrics for the last 15m
  • Cross-checked cluster state and recent deploys
  • Matched failure pattern: ephemeral-storage eviction
  • Drafted fix and validated against 3 sibling pods
deploy/log-ingester/values.yaml+4 −1
- ephemeralStorage: 1Gi+ ephemeralStorage: 4Gi+ logRotation:+   maxSize: 100Mi+   keep: 5
Root cause
Pod exceeded its ephemeral-storage: 1Gi limit due to log buildup. Kubelet evicted the pod, triggering restart loop. Sibling pods on the same node are trending toward the same threshold.
Confidence94%
Generate PRRun runbookPost to Slack
The problem

The Bigger Your Cloud Gets, the Harder It Is to Run.

More services. More clusters. More alerts. Every new layer of scale lands on the same small platform team.

Observability as an afterthought

Endpoints and services go live before anyone can really see them. Logs and metrics sit in different consoles.

Alert noise & paging fatigue

Alertmanager, Datadog, custom webhooks, all firing into one Slack channel with no correlation or routing.

Kubernetes complexity outpaces the team

CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, scheduling failures. Every incident becomes a kubectl scavenger hunt.

On-call burns out the best engineers

No structured rotations, no escalation paths, no audit trail. Your senior SRE is the human pager.

Cloud bills nobody can explain

Over-provisioned requests, idle EC2, forgotten RDS, while finance asks why spend doubled this quarter.

Tribal knowledge in shell history

Recovery steps live in someone's terminal scrollback. New engineers re-learn every fire drill.

GPU fleets are flying blind

CUDA OOM, NCCL timeouts, MIG slices unaccounted for. Half the H100s are idle while the other half throttle, and nobody can prove it.

ML pipelines stall silently

A Kubeflow DAG hangs at step 7. A vLLM replica drifts on p99. Nobody pages until the data scientist files a ticket.

Training runs you can't trust

Loss diverges, gradients NaN, dataloader stalls. You burn $14k of GPU hours before someone checks Weights & Biases.

Platform

One platform. Every operational surface.

30+ capabilities across observability, incident response, FinOps, automation, GPU & MLOps, and compliance — designed to feel like one product.

Kubernetes cluster overview

The Kubernetes hub: scope by cluster + duration, see active incidents (RESTART STORM, OOMKilled, ContainerCreation, rollout stuck), open View RCA on any card, and chat with the cluster Copilot, without leaving the page.

overviewincidentscopilotdrill-down

AlertMend AI chat

Ask cluster questions in plain language. Grounded in your real inventory, events, logs and metrics. Not generic suggestions.

natural-languagegrounded

Health rules

Per-cluster monitors for pods, nodes, PVCs, jobs, deploys. Severity, live state, operational toggle, without redeploying YAML.

workflow-poddeployavailability

Kubernetes logs (SQL)

SELECT * FROM logs with namespace/pod/node fields, time-range presets and stream selection.

sqlstreamsindexed

Metrics & dashboards

Custom panels, data-source selectors, auto-refresh, persisted via the workspace API. One source of truth across teams.

panelsworkspace-api

API monitors

Synthetic HTTP(S) checks with status, body or response-value rules. 90-day uptime ribbons. Wired into incidents & escalation paths.

syntheticuptimeslack

GPU & MLOps observability

First-class for NVIDIA fleets: per-GPU util, memory, ECC, thermal & MIG slices. Train-time signals from PyTorch DDP, Ray, Kubeflow, MLflow. Inference health for vLLM & Triton with KV-cache pressure and p99 drift detection.

cudancclvllmkubeflowmlflowright-size-gpu

Runbooks · visual workflows that act

Trigger on alerts, cron, health policy or webhooks. Run the same command across an entire VM fleet, or fan out into every pod that matches a label. Branch on output. Pause for Slack/Teams approval. Archive logs to S3, then post a closing summary to chat. Same definition, every time.

approvalvm-fleetk8s-fan-outslackteamsprometheusdatadogjirapagerduty

Kubernetes FinOps

Spend by namespace, workload, controller. Requested vs used. Apply recommended fix with YAML preview & cluster apply.

right-sizingyaml-apply

AWS cost optimization

EC2, RDS, ELB, ECS line items. Per-resource save $/mo, idle-instance cleanup, environment-scoped views.

ec2rdssavings

On-call programs

Schedules & rotations with timezones. Escalation paths chain email → WhatsApp → phone with wait timers. Sustainable paging at scale.

schedulesescalationwhatsapp

RBAC, audit & compliance, built in

Granular role-based access controls scope navigation, data and mutating actions. Every page navigation, click and apply is captured in the audit trail with per-user filters and error-only views, for security reviews, break-glass checks and SoC-style separation of duties.

Talk to security
Flagship · AI RCAs

Every Kubernetes alert gets a structured root cause in ~15 seconds.

A Kubernetes-specialized model auto-collects the evidence (pod YAML, container status, logs, node metrics, events), reaches a conclusion with confidence level, and posts the remediation steps directly into the Slack alert thread. It also runs proactively on failures the platform discovers itself: restart storms, OOM loops, scheduling stalls, even when no external alert paged.

  • Executive summary → Evidence → Conclusion → Remediation. Every report follows the same readable contract.
  • Bring your own model. Point inference at a local or self-hosted model for air-gapped, regulated or data-residency-sensitive environments.
  • Slack-native delivery + cluster-overview View RCA. Works for both alert-driven and proactively-detected incidents.
  • Deep links back into Pod metrics, Pod logs and resource details, so the responder jumps from narrative to live evidence.
See an RCA on your clusterLearn more
CRITICAL · RESTART STORMcluster: prod · ns: autosre
RCA · 14.8s
Resource
log-ingester-86b99687d7-cvklg
Executive summary
The pod is experiencing frequent restarts due to ephemeral-storage pressure on its node, disrupting log ingestion.
Evidence collected
› Pod YAML, eviction msg: "node low on ephemeral-storage"
› Container Status, 257 restarts, last reason: Error
› Pod Logs, pod did not live long enough to log
› Node metrics, no significant memory pressure detected
Conclusion · confidence: high
Ephemeral-storage exhaustion on the scheduling node is causing eviction loops on this workload.
Remediation
  1. Set explicit ephemeral-storage requests & limits on the pod
  2. Reschedule onto a node with larger ephemeral storage
  3. Add cluster-wide ephemeral-storage monitoring
Pod → MetricsPod → LogsPod → Details
Service-specialized AI

Not a generic LLM. Trained for the services you actually run.

Every service has its own failure-mode vocabulary. AlertMend ships purpose-built RCA models, runbook templates, and health rules for the databases, queues, proxies, caches, search engines, GPU fleets, distributed training frameworks, ML pipelines, and LLM inference servers that power production. So the AI knows what a CUDA OOM, a stuck Kubeflow DAG, a Mongo replica lag, and a Kafka ISR shrink each really mean.

MySQL
Replication & query intelligence
Replication lagSlow queriesDeadlocksConnection stormsBuffer pool pressure
PostgreSQL
WAL, vacuum, lock awareness
Replication slot bloatAutovacuum lagLock contentionLong-running txWAL exhaustion
MongoDB
Replica sets & sharding
Replica set lagOplog windowSlow queriesElection stormsChunk imbalance
Apache Kafka
Streaming & consumer health
Consumer lagISR shrinkPartition skewOffset commit failBroker disk pressure
Nginx
Edge & upstream intelligence
5xx spikesUpstream errorsWorker exhaustionTLS handshake failConn-limit hit
Redis
Cache & persistence patterns
Memory pressureEviction stormsAOF / RDB failReplication breakSlow commands
Elasticsearch / OpenSearch
Cluster, shard, query health
Heap pressureShard relocationSlow queriesYellow / red statusIndexing throttling
RabbitMQ
Queue depth & flow control
Queue depthChannel churnMemory alarmDisk-space alarmUnack messages
Cassandra
Compaction & read-path
Compaction backlogHint overloadTombstone scansRead repair stormsGC pauses
etcd
Raft & control-plane safety
Raft leader churnSlow applyDB size growthQuota exceededDisk fsync latency
HAProxy
L4 / L7 load balancer health
Backend downQueue depthConn-limit hitHealth-check failSSL errors
NVIDIA GPU
CUDA, MIG & nvidia-smi
CUDA OOMThermal throttlingECC errorsMIG slice exhaustIdle GPUs
PyTorch DDP
Distributed training & NCCL
NCCL timeoutLoss NaNGradient explodeDataloader stallCheckpoint fail
Ray
Distributed compute & RLlib
Head-node OOMActor restart loopObject spillAutoscaler stuckPlasma full
Kubeflow
ML pipelines on Kubernetes
Pipeline DAG failKFServing 5xxNotebook OOMKatib trial timeoutTFJob restart
MLflow
Experiment tracking & registry
Run write failArtifact uploadRegistry driftTracking server slowStale model
vLLM / Triton
LLM inference serving
p99 latency driftKV cache pressureBatch starvationModel load failOOM kill
Argo Workflows
Pipeline orchestration
Workflow stuckPod retry stormArtifact lostSuspended stepCron skip
Pinecone / Weaviate
Vector DB & retrieval
Index rebuildRecall dropQuery p99 spikeShard imbalanceEmbedding lag
Apache Airflow
Data & ML DAG scheduler
Scheduler heartbeatTask SLA missXCom oversizeDAG parse failWorker stall
Apache Spark
Batch & ML feature compute
Executor OOMShuffle skewDriver GCStage retry stormSlow task
Your service here
Custom training on request
VaultConsulClickHouseCockroachDBTemporalFlinkMilvusFeastTensorFlowJAX
Each service ships with its own failure-mode catalog, health-rule presets, runbook templates, and RCA prompts tuned to the way that service actually breaks in production.
Don't see your service? Tell us
GPU & ML pipelines

GPU clusters and ML pipelines, observed end-to-end.

From CUDA OOM on an H100 node to a stuck Kubeflow DAG to a p99 latency drift on your vLLM inference fleet, AlertMend understands the failure modes that actually break ML in production, then triages and remediates them like the rest of your stack.

GPU fleet
NVIDIA H100 / A100 · live nvidia-smi

util0%
mem4%

What we catch

The ML failure-mode catalog, baked in.

Every signal is mapped to a known failure pattern across the GPU stack, distributed training frameworks, pipeline orchestrators, model serving, vector databases and the registry. So your on-call gets a real diagnosis, not a Datadog graph.

GPU OOM
CUDA OOM kills, MIG slice exhaustion, mem fragmentation
Thermal throttling
SM clock drop, sustained >85°C, fan failures
NCCL collective fail
AllReduce timeout, ring break, node disconnect
Training divergence
NaN loss, vanishing/exploding grads, stuck epoch
Pipeline DAG stuck
Argo / Kubeflow / Airflow task retries, artifact loss
Inference latency drift
p99 spike, queue depth, dynamic batching off-target
Vector index health
Pinecone / Weaviate / Milvus shard rebuild, recall drop
Model registry drift
Stale model in prod, A/B variant skew, schema mismatch
Runbooks · No-code automation

Build your own AI workflows.

Drag-and-drop nodes for Kubernetes, AWS, Slack, Teams, Jira, PagerDuty, Prometheus, Datadog, REST APIs and your own webhooks. Conditional logic, human-in-the-loop approvals, fan-out across pods or VM fleets, and a full audit trail.

Auto-remediate · CrashLoopBackOffDisk pressure · VM fleetCost · right-size pods
ACTIVE
Trigger
Alert fired
CrashLoopBackOff · prod cluster
Command
Run pod diagnostic
kubectl describe + logs · last 5m
If / else
restarts > 5?
Branch on container status
Approval · Slack
Approve restart
@oncall · 5 min timeout
Send Message
Slack info post
#sre-ops · transient blip
Remediation
Rollout restart
Fan out · all pods · label tier=api
Closing summary
Post to Slack
What ran · who approved · audit link
Action blocks
CommandPredefinedDelayApprovalSend MessageDeclare IncidentGoogle MeetPrometheusVictoria MetricsDatadogPagerDutyJiraREST APIGitHub PRGitLab MRCloudWatch · soonAzure Monitor · soonGrafana · soon
8
Trigger sources

Alerts, cron, HealthPolicy, webhooks, chat slash-commands, custom integrations.

∞
Fleet fan-out

Run the same step on every connected VM, or every pod that matches a label selector.

3+
Approval channels

In-product, Slack, Microsoft Teams, Email. Pause until the right humans confirm.

100%
Audited

Every step, approval, command and output recorded for compliance and break-glass review.

How it works

The AIOps platform, end to end.

Four stages. One workspace. Connect what you already run, then automate the rest.

1

Connect

Register Agents for EC2 / ECS / CloudWatch, add VMs over SSH, and connect Kubernetes clusters. Invite teammates with RBAC.

2

Observe

Land on the cluster overview, drill into pods, query logs in SQL, build metric dashboards, watch APIs with synthetic checks.

3

Respond

Alerts → Incidents → On-call. Filter rules route signal to the right channel. AI RCAs land in the Slack thread automatically.

4

Automate & save

Runbooks run safe, approved fixes across pods or VM fleets. FinOps right-sizing applies YAML straight to the cluster.

Integrations

Wires into your stack. Doesn't replace it.

Ingest from Alertmanager, Datadog, Victoria Metrics, custom webhooks. Page through Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, WhatsApp, email, phone. File into Jira. Store in S3. Run anywhere.

Free cluster health check

Find your cluster's weak spots, before they page you.

On the call, AlertMend scans your live cluster end to end: workloads trending toward OOM, restart-prone pods, misconfigured limits, over-provisioned spend, unowned alerts. You walk away with a prioritized list of risks to fix, no incident required.

Book a demoTalk with us
Read-only scan Results in minutes No tool replacement required SOC-style RBAC & audit
AlertMend

Production operations for Kubernetes & AWS. Connect inventory, observe everything, automate the toil. AI-native. Works with your stack.
